overall_assessment: RR-AUTH-04 is non-blocking on the corrected baseline. The bounded resolver, reset-token, and risk-matrix changes are coherent and the cited tenant/admin regression coverage now proves the main fail-closed and single-use reset claims. The remaining critique debt is structural and evidentiary rather than release-blocking: tenant-public password governance still depends on route composition, and part of the cited impacted-auth spillover evidence preconditions the very auth posture it is presented to validate.
recommended_path: Proceed with the critique gate as non-blocking for RR-AUTH-04, but record two medium follow-up items before calling the slice fully clean: formalize tenant-public password governance beyond route middleware, and either narrow or explicitly waive the helper-driven impacted-auth suites as posture spillover evidence.

findings.title: Tenant-public password governance is still enforced at route composition instead of the application-service boundary
findings.rationale: The live tenant-public password routes are fail-closed because laravel-app/routes/api/public_tenant_maybe_api_v1.php:97-107 wraps login, register, reset-token issue, and reset-consume with EnsureTenantPublicAuthMethod, and the bounded feature tests prove those HTTP paths. But the underlying services in laravel-app/app/Application/Auth/AccountAuthenticationService.php:19-47 and laravel-app/app/Application/Profiles/TenantProfileService.php:85-140 do not consult tenant_public_auth, and laravel-app/tests/Unit/Application/Auth/AccountAuthenticationServiceTest.php:119-147 explicitly proves password login still succeeds while tenant_public_auth is pinned to phone_otp. The corrected HTTP surface is safe today, but a future route/controller/non-HTTP reuse could reopen password auth without changing the resolver or the package's cited fail-closed tests.
findings.suggested_action: Keep RR-AUTH-04 non-blocking, but formalize this boundary in follow-up: either move tenant-public password operations behind a dedicated facade that checks governance before login/reset work, or add a deterministic guardrail that rejects tenant-public password routes/controllers unless EnsureTenantPublicAuthMethod is present.

findings.title: Part of the cited impacted-auth spillover evidence force-enables password before exercising the affected flows
findings.rationale: The bounded package cites the impacted-auth consumer suite as supporting evidence, including tests/Api/v1/Tenants/Auth and tests/Api/v1/Accounts/Auth. However, laravel-app/tests/Api/Traits/AccountAuthFunctions.php:38-63 force-sets tenant_public_auth to ['password'] before login requests, laravel-app/tests/Api/v1/Accounts/Auth/Contracts/ApiV1AccountAuthTestContract.php:18-24 seeds the same fixture in setUp, and laravel-app/tests/Api/v1/Tenants/Auth/Contracts/ApiV1PasswordRegistrationTestContract.php:25-33 does likewise for tenant password-registration coverage. Those suites still provide useful password-path regression coverage, but they cannot independently prove RR-AUTH-04 consumer compatibility under the production default posture because they precondition the exact auth setting this slice hardens.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the helper-driven suites as secondary auth regression coverage, but do not present them as primary RR-AUTH-04 posture spillover proof unless a separate unaffected lane exercises the same consumers without mutating tenant_public_auth, or the TODO/package explicitly records this precondition as an accepted evidence limitation.
